passages.title: Stress of Workplace
passages.source_html:
Stress of Workplace 


A 


How busy is too busy? For some it means having to miss the occasional long lunch; for others, it means missing lunch altogether. For a few, it is not being able to take a ‘sickie’ once a month. Then there is a group of people for whom working every evening and weekend is normal, and frantic is the tempo of their lives. For most senior executives, workloads swing between extremely busy and frenzied. The vice-president of the management consultancy AT Kearney and its head of telecommunications for the Asia-Pacific region, Neil Plumridge, says his work weeks vary from a ‘manageable’ 45 hours to 80 hours, but average 60 hours. 


B 


Three warning signs alert Plumridge about his workload: sleep, scheduling and family. He knows he has too much on when he gets less than six hours of sleep for three consecutive nights; when he is constantly having to reschedule appointments; ‘and the third one is on the family side,’ says Plumridge, the father of a three-year-old daughter, and expecting a second child in October. ‘If I happen to miss a birthday or anniversary, I know things are out of control.’ Being ‘too busy’ is highly subjective. But for any individual, the perception of being too busy over a prolonged period can start showing up as stress: disturbed sleep, and declining mental and physical health. National workers’ compensation figures show stress causes the most lost time of any workplace injury. Employees suffering stress are off work an average of 16.6 weeks. The effects of stress are also expensive. Comcare, the Federal Government insurer, reports that in 2003-04, claims for psychological injury accounted for 7% of claims but almost 27% of claim costs. Experts say the key to dealing with stress is not to focus on relief – a game of golf or a massage – but to reassess workloads. Neil Plumridge says he makes it a priority to work out what has to change; that might mean allocating extra resources to a job, allowing more time or changing expectations. The decision may take several days. He also relies on the advice of colleagues, saying his peers’ coach each other with business problems. “Just a fresh pair of eyes over an issue can help,” he says. 


C 


Executive stress is not confined to big organisations. Vanessa Stoykov has been running her own advertising and public relations business for seven years, specializing in work for financial and professional services firms. Evolution Media has grown so fast that it debuted on the BRW Fast 100 list of fastest-growing small enterprises last year – just after Stoykov had her first child. Stoykov thrives on the mental stimulation of running her own business. ‘Like everyone, I have the occasional day when I think my head’s going to blow off,’ she says. Because of the growth phase, the business is in, Stoykov has to concentrate on short-term stress relief – weekends in the mountains, the occasional “mental health” day – rather than delegating more work. She says: ‘We’re hiring more people, but you need to train them, teach them about the culture and the clients, so it’s actually more work rather than less.’ 


D 


Identify the causes: Jan Elsnera, Melbourne psychologist who specialises in executive coaching, says thriving on a demanding workload is typical of senior executives and other high-potential business people. She says there is no one-size-fits-all approach to stress: some people work best with high-adrenalin periods followed by quieter patches, while others thrive under sustained pressure. ‘We could take urine and blood hormonal measures and pass judgement of whether someone’s physiologically stressed or not,’ she says, ‘But that’s not going to give us an indicator of what their experience of stress is, and what the emotional and cognitive impacts of stress are going to be.’ 


E 


Elsner’s practice is informed by a movement known as positive psychology, a school of thought that argues ‘positive’ experiences – feeling engaged, challenged, and that one is making a contribution to something meaningful – do not balance out negative ones such as stress; instead, they help people increase their resilience over time. Good stress, or positive experiences of being challenged and rewarded, is thus cumulative in the same way as bad stress. Elsner says many of the senior business people she coaches are relying more on regulating bad stress through methods such as meditation and yoga. She points to research showing that meditation can alter the biochemistry of the brain and actually help people “retrain” the way their brains and bodies react to stress. ‘Meditation and yoga enable you to shift the way that your brain reacts, so if you get proficient at it you’re in control.’ 


F 


The Australian vice-president of AT Kearney, Neil Plumridge, says: ‘Often stress is caused by our setting unrealistic expectations of ourselves. I’ll promise a client I’ll do something tomorrow, and the [promise] another client the same thing, when I really know it’s not going to happen. I’ve put stress on myself when I could have said to the clients: “Why don’t I give that to you in 48 hours?” The client doesn’t care.’ Overcommitting is something people experience as an individual problem. We explain it as the result of procrastination or Parkinson’s law: that work expands to fill the time available. New research indicates that people may be hard-wired to do it. 


G 


A study in the February issue of the Journal of Experimental Psychology shows that people always believe they will be less busy in the future than now. This is a misapprehension, according to the authors of the report, Professor Gal Zauberman, of the University of North Carolina, and Professor John Lynch, of Duke University. ‘On average, an individual will be just as busy two weeks or a month from now as he or she is today. But that is not how it appears to be in everyday life,’ they wrote, ‘People often make commitments long in advance that they would never make if the same commitments required immediate action. That is, they discount future time investments relatively steeply.’ Why do we perceive a greater ‘surplus’ of time in the future than in the present? The researchers suggest that people underestimate completion times for tasks stretching into the future, and that they are bad at imagining future competition for their time.

groups.title: Questions 14-27
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-18 


Use the information in the passage to match the people (listed A-D) with opinions or deeds below. Write the appropriate letters A-D in boxes 14-18 on your answer sheet. 


NB You may use any letter more than once.


A Jan Elsnera 

B Vanessa Stoykov 

C Gal Zauberman 

D Neil Plumridge 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 Work stress usually happens in the high level of a business.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Identify the causes: Jan Elsnera, Melbourne psychologist who specialises in executive coaching, says thriving on a demanding workload is typical of senior executives and other high-potential business people. 

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问“工作压力通常发生在企业的高层”。  

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"Work stress"和"high level of a business"定位到文章中关于工作压力和企业层级的句子。  

解题思路：原文C段提到"Executive stress is not confined to big organisations."（高管压力不仅仅只存在于大公司），说明高管压力是高层普遍存在的现象。题目中的"high level of a business"对应"Executive stress"，而"Executive"通常指高层管理人员。A选项的Jan Elsnera在D段又提到"thriving on a demanding workload is typical of senior executives"（高层管理人员通常能应付繁重的工作），进一步验证了高管压力的存在。因此，答案为A。
15

15 More people’s ideas involved would be beneficial for stress relief
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：He also relies on the advice of colleagues, saying his peers’ coach each other with business problems. “Just a fresh pair of eyes over an issue can help,” he says. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是“更多的人的想法有助于缓解压力”，需要找到与多人参与或建议相关的观点。

定位关键信息：通过关键词“stress relief”和“more people’s ideas”定位到B段，其中提到Neil Plumridge依靠同事的建议来处理问题，强调“just a fresh pair of eyes over an issue can help”，即他人的观点有助于解决问题。

解题思路：原文中Neil Plumridge提到自己通过同事的建议来缓解压力，说明多人参与或他人观点的介入对缓解压力有益。因此，这一观点与题目要求一致，对应选项D。
16

16 Temporary holiday sometimes doesn’t mean less work.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Because of the growth phase, the business is in, Stoykov has to concentrate on short-term stress relief – weekends in the mountains, the occasional “mental health” day – rather than delegating more work.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问“临时休假有时并不意味着工作量减少”，即休假或短暂休息并不能真正减轻工作负担。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的“Temporary holiday”（临时休假）和“doesn’t mean less work”（不意味着工作量减少）定位。原文中C段提到Stoykov通过“short-term stress relief”（短期减压）如“weekends in the mountains”（去山里度周末）和“occasional ‘mental health’ day”（偶尔的“心理健康日”）来缓解压力，但明确说明这些措施是“rather than delegating more work”（而不是分配更多工作），表明休假并未真正减少工作量。

解题思路：原文C段描述Stoykov因业务增长需要短期减压，但并未通过分配工作来减轻负担，而是通过休假等方式暂时缓解。题干中的“Temporary holiday sometimes doesn’t mean less work”与原文中“short-term stress relief”和“rather than delegating more work”对应，说明休假并未真正减少工作量，因此答案为B（Stoykov）。
17

17 Stress leads to a wrong direction when trying to satisfy customers.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Neil Plumridge, says: ‘Often stress is caused by our setting unrealistic expectations of ourselves. I’ll promise a client I’ll do something tomorrow, and the [promise] another client the same thing, when I really know it’s not going to happen. I’ve put stress on myself when I could have said to the clients: “Why don’t I give that to you in 48 hours?” The client doesn’t care.’

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“压力导致在试图满足客户时走向错误的方向”，即压力如何影响满足客户的行为。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“stress”和“satisfy customers”定位到F段，该段讨论了压力对不同人的影响。

解题思路：原文中Plumridge提到，有时压力来自于我们对自己不切实际的期待，他有时会答应客户明天做好某件事，接着又去答应另一客户，明天也会做好某件事，对应题目中“satisfy customers”，但他明明知道自己无法做到，给了自己不切实际的期待，又接着说明明可以直接对客户说48小时后再完成，可见他不赞成过度承诺，这是“wrong direction”。因此，D是正确答案。
18

18 It is not correct that stress in the future will be eased more than now. It is more accurate than candles and oil lamps.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：A study in the February issue of the Journal of Experimental Psychology shows that people always believe they will be less busy in the future than now. This is a misapprehension, according to the authors of the report, Professor Gal Zauberman, of the University of North Carolina, and Professor John Lynch, of Duke University. 

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“未来压力不会比现在少”这一观点出自于谁。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"stress in the future"和"eased more than now"，通过这些词可以定位到G段中关于人们总是认为未来会比现在更不忙的描述。

解题思路：G段明确提到，研究表明，人们总是认为未来会比现在更轻松，对应题目中“stress in the future will be eased more than now”，但Zauberman说，这是一种误解。与题目中的信息对应，因此答案是C。

Questions 19-21 


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D. Write the correct letter in boxes 19-21 on your answer sheet.


19

Which of the following workplace stress is NOT mentioned according to Plumridge in the following options?

正确答案： B. Unable to concentrate on work

原文答案句：Three warning signs alert Plumridge about his workload: sleep, scheduling and family. He knows he has too much on when he gets less than six hours of sleep for three consecutive nights; when he is constantly having to reschedule appointments; ‘and the third one is on the family side,’ says Plumridge, the father of a three-year-old daughter, and expecting a second child in October. ‘If I happen to miss a birthday or anniversary, I know things are out of control.’

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问根据Plumridge的说法，以下哪一项工作压力未被提及。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"Plumridge"和"workplace stress"定位到B段，该段详细描述了Plumridge判断工作压力过大的三个信号。

解题思路：根据B段原文，Plumridge提到的三个压力信号分别是：睡眠不足（"less than six hours of sleep"对应选项C）、频繁改约（"constantly having to reschedule appointments"对应选项D）、家庭时间不足（"miss a birthday or anniversary"对应选项A）。选项B“无法集中精力工作”在原文中未被提及，因此B是正确答案。


20

Which of the following solution is NOT mentioned in helping reduce the work pressure according to Plumridge?

正确答案： D. Do sports and massage

原文答案句：Neil Plumridge says he makes it a priority to work out what has to change; that might mean allocating extra resources to a job, allowing more time or changing expectations. He also relies on the advice of colleagues, saying his peers’ coach each other with business problems. “Just a fresh pair of eyes over an issue can help,” he says.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是根据Plumridge的说法，以下哪个解决方案在帮助减轻工作压力方面没有被提及。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"Plumridge"和"reduce work pressure"，可以定位到B段中Plumridge关于如何处理工作压力的描述。

解题思路：根据B段中的原文，Plumridge提到的解决方案包括分配更多资源，也就是让更多人去做某项工作（对应A选项）、延长完成时间（对应B选项）和调整预期目标（对应C选项）。然而，原文中并未提及通过做运动和按摩来减轻工作压力（对应D选项），因此D选项是正确答案。


21

What is point of view of Jan Elsnera towards work stress?

正确答案： A. Medical test can only reveal part of the data needed to cope with stress

原文答案句：We could take urine and blood hormonal measures and pass judgement of whether someone’s physiologically stressed or not,’ she says, ‘But that’s not going to give us an indicator of what their experience of stress is, and what the emotional and cognitive impacts of stress are going to be.’

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是Jan Elsnera对工作压力的观点是什么。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"Jan Elsnera"和"work stress"可以定位到D段，该段是关于Jan Elsnera对工作压力的看法。

解题思路：根据D段中的原文答案句，Jan Elsnera提到通过尿液和血液激素测量只能判断一个人生理上是否压力过大，但无法给出一个人经历压力的指标，以及压力对情感和认知的影响。这意味着她认为医疗测试只能揭示应对压力所需数据的一部分，而情感和认知影响是更重要的。因此，选项A"Medical test can only reveal part of the data needed to cope with stress"最符合她的观点。


Questions 22-27 


Complete the summary below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 22-27 on your answer sheet.


22

Statistics from National worker’s compensation indicate stress plays the most important role in 22  which cause the time losses.

正确答案： 22 workplace injury
Question 22

原文答案句：National workers’ compensation figures show stress causes the most lost time of any workplace injury.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是全国工伤赔偿的统计数据表明，压力在什么中，是工作时间减少的最主要因素。

定位关键信息：题目中的"Statistics from National worker’s compensation"和"stress plays the most important role in"是定位的关键信息。通过这些关键词，我们可以在文章中找到相关的句子。

解题思路：在B段中，我们可以找到这样一句话："National workers’ compensation figures show stress causes the most lost time of any workplace injury."这句话表明，国家工人赔偿的统计数据表明，任何工伤中，压力导致的工作时间损失最多。所以，答案是"workplace injury"。


23

Staffs take about 23 for absence from work caused by stress.

正确答案： 23 16.6 weeks
Question 23

原文答案句：National workers’ compensation figures show stress causes the most lost time of any workplace injury. Employees suffering stress are off work an average of 16.6 weeks.

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目询问员工因压力导致的缺勤平均时长是多少。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“absence from work caused by stress”可以定位到B段中讨论压力造成工时损失的句子。  

解题思路：题目中的“absence from work”与原文中的“are off work”为同义替换。原文明确给出了因压力而缺勤的员工平均休假时长是“16.6 weeks”。这个数字直接回答了题目关于缺勤时长的问题，因此确定答案为“16.6 weeks”。


24

Not just time is our main concern but great expenses generated consequently. 

An official insurer wrote sometime that about 24 of all claims were mental issues whereas nearly 27% costs in all claims.

正确答案： 24 7%
Question 24

原文答案句：Comcare, the Federal Government insurer, reports that in 2003-04, claims for psychological injury accounted for 7% of claims but almost 27% of claim costs.

对应段落：B段

审题： 题目指出，我们关心的不仅是时间，还有由此产生的巨大开支。一家联邦政府保险公司曾报告道，在所有索赔中，约有多少是心理问题，而却占了所有索赔费用的近27%。需要填入心理问题索赔所占的百分比。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“official insurer”、“claims”、“mental issues”和“27% costs”可以定位到B段中关于Comcare公司索赔数据和成本的句子。

解题思路：题目中的“official insurer”对应原文的“Comcare, the Federal Government insurer”，“mental issues”对应“psychological injury”。原文句子结构清晰地给出了两个百分比：心理伤害索赔的数量“占总索赔的7%”，但其成本“占总索赔成本的近27%”。题目空格要求填入的是索赔数量的占比，与原文的“7% of claims”对应。因此，答案为“7%”。


25

Sports such as 25 ,

正确答案： 25 golf
Question 25

原文答案句：Experts say the key to dealing with stress is not to focus on relief – a game of golf or a massage – but to reassess workloads.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目询问哪种运动可以作为缓解压力的方法。

定位关键信息： 通过题目中“sports”和“treatment to release stress”可以定位到B段中讨论缓解压力的方法的部分。

解题思路：原文在提到缓解压力（relief）时，明确列举了“a game of golf or a massage”作为例子。其中，“golf”是一项运动，与题目中的“sports”直接对应。因此，高尔夫是作为一种缓解压力的方式被提及的，确定答案为“golf”。


26

as well as 26 could be a treatment to release stress;

正确答案： 26 massage
Question 26

原文答案句： Experts say the key to dealing with stress is not to focus on relief – a game of golf or a massage – but to reassess workloads.

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目询问除了运动之外，还有什么活动可以作为缓解压力的方法。 

定位关键信息：通 通过题目中“as well as”和“treatment to release stress”可以定位到B段中与“golf”并列的缓解压力的方式。

解题思路：原文在破折号中列举了两种缓解压力的方式：“a game of golf”和“a massage”。题目中的“as well as”表示并列关系，因此26题需要填入与“golf”并列的“massage”。确定答案为“massage”。

27

However, specialists recommended another practical way out, analyse 27 once again.

正确答案： 27 workloads
Question 27

原文答案句： Experts say the key to dealing with stress is not to focus on relief – a game of golf or a massage – but to reassess workloads.

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目询问专家推荐的实际解决方法是什么，即需要重新分析什么。

定位关键信息：通过题目中“specialists recommended another practical way out”和“analyse...once again”可以定位到B段中专家提出的关键建议。

解题思路：原文使用“not...but...”的结构，明确指出应对压力的关键“不是”专注于缓解（如高尔夫或按摩），“而是”要重新评估（reassess） workloads。题目中的“analyse...once again”是“reassess”的同义转述。因此，专家推荐的方法是重新分析“workloads”。确定答案为“workloads”。
